title: Minnesota Bill Directs Study on SAF Production from Wastewater COâ‚‚ and Green Hydrogen
content: The Minnesota Legislature has introduced a bill directing the Pollution Control Agency to study the feasibility and cost of producing sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) using carbon dioxide captured from wastewater sludge incineration and green hydrogen. The initiative includes designing carbon capture infrastructure, conducting a front-end engineering study for SAF production in the metropolitan area, and reporting by June 30, 2029, on projected costs, job creation, and SAF output potential. A one-time appropriation of $300,000 is allocated to support this effort.
author: Analysts Account
